authors.name: Frank Herbert
authors.bio: American science-fiction author best known for Dune.

authors.name: J.R.R. Tolkien
authors.bio: English writer and philologist, author of The Lord of the Rings.

authors.name: Agatha Christie
authors.bio: English writer known for detective novels featuring Poirot and Miss Marple.

authors.name: Isaac Asimov
authors.bio: American author and professor of biochemistry, prolific in science fiction.

authors.name: Ursula K. Le Guin
authors.bio: American author of novels, short stories, and essays in speculative fiction.

authors.name: Neil Gaiman
authors.bio: English author of short fiction, novels, comic books, and films.

authors.name: Terry Pratchett
authors.bio: English humorist, satirist, and author of the Discworld fantasy series.

authors.name: George Orwell
authors.bio: English novelist and essayist, known for dystopian and political writing.

authors.name: Jane Austen
authors.bio: English novelist known for social commentary and realism in Regency England.

authors.name: Philip K. Dick
authors.bio: American science fiction writer who explored altered realities and identity.

authors.name: Stephen King
authors.bio: American author of horror, supernatural fiction, and suspense.

authors.name: Arthur C. Clarke
authors.bio: British science fiction writer and futurist, co-writer of 2001: A Space Odyssey.

authors.name: Margaret Atwood
authors.bio: Canadian poet, novelist, and literary critic.

authors.name: Kurt Vonnegut
authors.bio: American writer known for blending satire, dark comedy, and science fiction.

authors.name: Ray Bradbury
authors.bio: American author and screenwriter who worked in fantasy, horror, and mystery.

authors.name: Fyodor Dostoevsky
authors.bio: Russian novelist and philosopher, one of the greatest literary figures.

authors.name: Gabriel Garcia Marquez
authors.bio: Colombian novelist and Nobel Prize laureate, pioneer of magical realism.

authors.name: Toni Morrison
authors.bio: American novelist and Nobel Prize laureate, known for exploring Black identity.

authors.name: Haruki Murakami
authors.bio: Japanese writer whose surreal fiction blends mundane and fantastical elements.

authors.name: Octavia Butler
authors.bio: American science fiction author who explored race, gender, and humanity.

authors.name: Douglas Adams
authors.bio: English author and humourist, best known for The Hitchhiker's Guide.

authors.name: Mary Shelley
authors.bio: English novelist who wrote Frankenstein at age 18, a foundational sci-fi text.

authors.name: H.P. Lovecraft
authors.bio: American writer of weird fiction and cosmic horror.

authors.name: Aldous Huxley
authors.bio: English writer and philosopher, author of Brave New World.

authors.name: Cormac McCarthy
authors.bio: American novelist known for sparse prose and violent, bleak narratives.

authors.name: Jorge Luis Borges
authors.bio: Argentine short-story writer and poet, central figure in Spanish literature.

authors.name: Chinua Achebe
authors.bio: Nigerian novelist, poet, and critic, author of Things Fall Apart.

authors.name: Virginia Woolf
authors.bio: English writer and modernist, pioneer of the stream-of-consciousness style.

authors.name: Ernest Hemingway
authors.bio: American novelist and journalist, known for economical and understated style.

authors.name: Franz Kafka
authors.bio: German-speaking Bohemian novelist known for surreal and existential fiction.

authors.name: William Gibson
authors.bio: American-Canadian speculative fiction writer, coined the term 'cyberspace'.

authors.name: N.K. Jemisin
authors.bio: American speculative fiction author, first to win three consecutive Hugo Awards.

authors.name: Liu Cixin
authors.bio: Chinese science fiction writer, author of The Three-Body Problem.

authors.name: Daphne du Maurier
authors.bio: English author and playwright known for gothic and suspenseful novels.

authors.name: Patrick Rothfuss
authors.bio: American author of epic fantasy, known for The Kingkiller Chronicle.

authors.name: Brandon Sanderson
authors.bio: American fantasy and science fiction writer known for intricate magic systems.

authors.name: Robin Hobb
authors.bio: American author of epic fantasy, known for the Realm of the Elderlings series.

authors.name: Joe Abercrombie
authors.bio: British fantasy author known for grimdark fiction and The First Law trilogy.

authors.name: Donna Tartt
authors.bio: American author known for dense literary novels published decades apart.

authors.name: Carl Sagan
authors.bio: American astronomer, cosmologist, and science communicator.

books.title: Dune
books.description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune tells the story of Paul Atreides as he navigates political intrigue, ecological challenges, and a prophecy that could change the universe.

books.title: The Fellowship of the Ring
books.description: A hobbit named Frodo inherits a powerful ring and must journey across Middle-earth to destroy it in the fires of Mount Doom before the Dark Lord Sauron can reclaim it.

books.title: Murder on the Orient Express
books.description: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates a murder aboard the luxurious Orient Express, where every passenger has a motive and an alibi.

books.title: Foundation
books.description: Mathematician Hari Seldon predicts the fall of the Galactic Empire and establishes the Foundation to preserve knowledge and shorten the coming dark age.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.description: An envoy from Earth visits the planet Gethen, whose inhabitants can change gender, challenging assumptions about identity, politics, and trust.

books.title: Good Omens
books.description: An angel and a demon who have grown fond of Earth team up to prevent the apocalypse in this comedic tale of good, evil, and everything in between.

books.title: American Gods
books.description: Shadow Moon discovers that the gods of old mythology live among us in America, and a war is brewing between them and the new gods of technology and media.

books.title: The Color of Magic
books.description: The first adventure on Terry Pratchett's Discworld, a flat world balanced on the backs of four elephants standing on a giant turtle, following the hapless wizard Rincewind.
In a totalitarian society where Big Brother watches everything, Winston Smith begins to question the Party and risks everything for truth and freedom.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.description: A group of farm animals overthrow their human farmer, only to find that their new leaders become indistinguishable from the tyrants they replaced.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of manners, morality, and marriage in Georgian England as she clashes with and ultimately falls for the proud Mr. Darcy.

books.title: Sense and Sensibility
books.description: Two sisters, one rational and one emotional, navigate love, heartbreak, and societal expectations in late-18th-century England.

books.title: Emma
books.description: Emma Woodhouse, a well-meaning but meddlesome young woman, attempts to play matchmaker for her friends with comic and sometimes disastrous results.

books.title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
books.description: In a post-apocalyptic world, bounty hunter Rick Deckard must retire six escaped androids while questioning what it means to be truly human.

books.title: A Scanner Darkly
books.description: An undercover agent becomes so dependent on the drug he is investigating that he can no longer distinguish his true identity from his assumed one.

books.title: The Man in the High Castle
books.description: In an alternate history where the Axis powers won World War II, Americans live under German and Japanese rule while a mysterious novel imagines Allied victory.

books.title: The Shining
books.description: Jack Torrance takes a winter caretaker job at the Overlook Hotel with his family, where supernatural forces and his own demons slowly drive him toward violence.

books.title: It
books.description: Seven children in Derry, Maine confront a shape-shifting evil that emerges every 27 years, then return as adults to finish what they started.

books.title: The Stand
books.description: After a superflu wipes out most of humanity, survivors are drawn to two opposing leaders in a final battle between good and evil.

books.title: Misery
books.description: After a car accident, novelist Paul Sheldon is rescued by his self-proclaimed number one fan, Annie Wilkes, who holds him captive and forces him to write.

books.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
books.description: A mysterious monolith guides humanity from ape to spacefarer, culminating in astronaut Dave Bowman's transcendent encounter beyond Jupiter.

books.title: Rendezvous with Rama
books.description: A massive cylindrical spacecraft enters the solar system, and a team of explorers boards it to discover an eerily empty alien world of engineered wonders.

books.title: Childhood's End
books.description: Benevolent alien Overlords bring peace to Earth, but their true purpose involves a transformation of humanity that will render the species unrecognizable.

books.title: The Handmaid's Tale
books.description: In the theocratic Republic of Gilead, women are stripped of all rights. Offred, a Handmaid, navigates survival and memory in a regime built on subjugation.

books.title: Oryx and Crake
books.description: Snowman may be the last true human alive, surrounded by genetically engineered creatures, remembering the brilliant and dangerous friend who brought the world to ruin.

books.title: Slaughterhouse-Five
books.description: Billy Pilgrim becomes unstuck in time, experiencing moments from his life out of order, including the firebombing of Dresden and his abduction by aliens.

books.title: Cat's Cradle
books.description: A writer researching the atomic bomb discovers ice-nine, a substance that could freeze all water on Earth, in this darkly comic exploration of science and faith.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.description: In a future where books are banned and firemen burn them, Guy Montag begins to question his role after meeting a young woman who shows him a world of ideas.

books.title: The Martian Chronicles
books.description: A series of interconnected stories chronicling humanity's colonization of Mars, the displacement of Martians, and the loneliness of the human condition.

books.title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
books.description: Two thirteen-year-old boys confront the sinister Mr. Dark and his nightmarish carnival that arrives in their small town offering terrible wishes fulfilled.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.description: Raskolnikov, a destitute former student, murders a pawnbroker and is consumed by guilt, paranoia, and a philosophical struggle over whether he is above moral law.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.description: Three brothers grapple with their father's murder while confronting questions of faith, free will, morality, and the nature of God in tsarist Russia.

books.title: Notes from Underground
books.description: A bitter, isolated narrator delivers a rambling monologue about consciousness, free will, and the perverse pleasure of refusing to be rational.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.description: Seven generations of the Buendia family live, love, and repeat history in the mythical town of Macondo in this landmark of magical realism.

books.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
books.description: Florentino Ariza waits over fifty years to pursue his love for Fermina Daza, finally reuniting with her after her husband's death in this meditation on love and time.

books.title: Beloved
books.description: Sethe, an escaped slave, is haunted by the ghost of her dead daughter in this wrenching exploration of the lasting trauma of slavery and the power of memory.

books.title: Song of Solomon
books.description: Milkman Dead embarks on a journey to discover his family's history, uncovering secrets that connect him to an ancestral legacy of flight and freedom.

books.title: Norwegian Wood
books.description: Toru Watanabe reflects on his college years in 1960s Tokyo, torn between two very different women as he navigates loss, desire, and the passage to adulthood.

books.title: Kafka on the Shore
books.description: A fifteen-year-old runaway and an elderly man who can talk to cats find their stories converging in this dreamlike tale of fate, identity, and memory.

books.title: 1Q84
books.description: In an alternate 1984 Tokyo, a fitness instructor and an aspiring writer are drawn together across parallel realities shaped by a mysterious cult and two moons.

books.title: Kindred
books.description: A modern Black woman is repeatedly pulled back in time to antebellum Maryland, where she must ensure the survival of a white slaveholder who is her ancestor.

books.title: Parable of the Sower
books.description: In a near-future California ravaged by climate change and inequality, young Lauren Olamina develops a new belief system and leads a community northward.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.description: Arthur Dent escapes Earth's demolition with his alien friend Ford Prefect and hitchhikes across the galaxy discovering the answer to life, the universe, and everything.

books.title: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe
books.description: Arthur Dent and friends visit Milliways, the restaurant at the end of the universe, where diners watch the cosmos end while enjoying a nice meal.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.description: Victor Frankenstein creates a living being from dead tissue and is horrified by the result, setting off a tragic chain of rejection, vengeance, and remorse.

books.title: At the Mountains of Madness
books.description: An Antarctic expedition discovers the ruins of a vast alien city and evidence of beings that predate humanity, with horrors that defy comprehension lurking deeper still.

books.title: The Call of Cthulhu
books.description: A series of documents and accounts reveal the existence of Cthulhu, a monstrous entity sleeping beneath the Pacific, worshipped by cults across the world.

books.title: Brave New World
books.description: In a world of engineered happiness, genetic castes, and mandatory pleasure, Bernard Marx begins to question the cost of a society that has abolished suffering.

books.title: Blood Meridian
books.description: A teenage runaway joins a gang of scalp hunters in the 1850s borderlands, led by the terrifying Judge Holden, in this unflinching meditation on violence and human nature.

books.title: The Road
books.description: A father and son push a shopping cart through a scorched, ashen America, scavenging for food and avoiding marauders in a world that has lost nearly everything.

books.title: No Country for Old Men
books.description: When Llewelyn Moss finds two million dollars at a drug deal gone wrong, he sets off a chain of violence as the unstoppable Anton Chigurh hunts him down.

books.title: Ficciones
books.description: A collection of short stories exploring infinite libraries, forking paths, imaginary encyclopedias, and the blurred boundaries between fiction and reality.

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.description: Okonkwo, a respected leader in an Igbo village, watches his world unravel as British missionaries and colonial government arrive in 1890s Nigeria.

books.title: Mrs Dalloway
books.description: Over the course of a single day in post-war London, Clarissa Dalloway prepares for a party while the narrative weaves through her memories and the lives of those around her.

books.title: To the Lighthouse
books.description: The Ramsay family and their guests visit a summer home on the Isle of Skye, and over ten years, loss and time transform their plans to visit a nearby lighthouse.

books.title: The Old Man and the Sea
books.description: An aging Cuban fisherman hooks a giant marlin far out in the Gulf Stream and struggles for days to bring it home, only to lose his prize to sharks.

books.title: A Farewell to Arms
books.description: An American ambulance driver on the Italian front during World War I falls in love with a British nurse, and together they try to escape the madness of war.

books.title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
books.description: Robert Jordan, an American fighting with Spanish guerrillas, has three days to blow up a bridge while falling in love with a young woman named Maria.

books.title: The Trial
books.description: Josef K. is arrested one morning for an unspecified crime and spends the rest of his life navigating a bewildering, opaque legal system that offers no answers.

books.title: The Metamorphosis
books.description: Gregor Samsa wakes up one morning transformed into a giant insect and must cope with his family's horror, his own isolation, and the loss of his humanity.

books.title: Neuromancer
books.description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job: to break into a powerful artificial intelligence, in this genre-defining cyberpunk novel.

books.title: Count Zero
books.description: Three storylines converge in a world of corporate espionage, sentient AI, and voodoo-practicing cyberspace cowboys in Gibson's Sprawl trilogy sequel.

books.title: The Fifth Season
books.description: On a continent racked by catastrophic seismic events, a woman with the power to control earthquakes searches for her kidnapped daughter while civilization crumbles.

books.title: The Obelisk Gate
books.description: As the world endures a devastating Season, Essun shelters in an underground community while her daughter Nassun discovers the true nature of orogeny.

books.title: The Stone Sky
books.description: Mother and daughter converge on opposite sides of a conflict that will determine whether the world is saved or destroyed in this conclusion to the Broken Earth trilogy.

books.title: The Three-Body Problem
books.description: During China's Cultural Revolution, a secret military project sends signals into space, making first contact with an alien civilization on the brink of destruction.

books.title: The Dark Forest
books.description: Earth prepares for an alien invasion four centuries away while grappling with the Dark Forest theory: the universe is full of hunters, and silence means survival.

books.title: Death's End
books.description: Spanning billions of years, the conclusion to the Remembrance of Earth's Past trilogy explores the ultimate fate of civilization in a hostile universe.

books.title: Rebecca
books.description: A young bride moves into the grand estate Manderley, haunted by the shadow of her husband's glamorous first wife, Rebecca, whose presence pervades everything.

books.title: My Cousin Rachel
books.description: Philip Ashley suspects his beautiful cousin Rachel of poisoning his guardian, but as he falls under her spell, he can no longer distinguish love from manipulation.

books.title: The Name of the Wind
books.description: Kvothe, a legendary figure now living in obscurity, tells the true story of his life — from orphaned trouper's child to the most notorious wizard of his age.

books.title: The Wise Man's Fear
books.description: Kvothe continues his story: searching for the mysterious Amyr, studying with a legendary swordsman, and surviving encounters with the fae.

books.title: The Way of Kings
books.description: On a world of stone and storms, Kaladin struggles as a slave soldier, Shallan seeks forbidden knowledge, and Dalinar pursues visions of ancient knights.

books.title: Words of Radiance
books.description: Shallan races to prevent a catastrophe while Kaladin discovers the extent of his powers and Dalinar faces assassination and political betrayal.

books.title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
books.description: In a world of ash and darkness ruled by an immortal emperor, a crew of thieves with metal-based magic powers plan the ultimate heist: overthrowing the empire.

books.title: Assassin's Apprentice
books.description: Young Fitz, the illegitimate son of a prince, is trained as a royal assassin while developing a forbidden telepathic bond with animals.

books.title: Royal Assassin
books.description: Fitz returns to court to find treachery everywhere as he serves his ailing king while the pretender Prince Regal plots to seize the throne.

books.title: The Blade Itself
books.description: A crippled torturer, a vain barbarian, and a disgraced nobleman are drawn into a web of war and intrigue in this darkly funny grimdark fantasy.

books.title: Before They Are Hanged
books.description: Glokta defends a besieged city, Jezal leads a doomed expedition to the edge of the world, and Logen faces his violent past.

books.title: Last Argument of Kings
books.description: War engulfs the Union as all plots converge, alliances shatter, and every character pays the price for the choices they have made.

books.title: The Secret History
books.description: A group of elite classics students at a Vermont college commit a murder inspired by Dionysian ritual, and their secret slowly destroys them from within.

books.title: The Goldfinch
books.description: After his mother dies in a bombing at the Metropolitan Museum, thirteen-year-old Theo Decker clings to a small painting that becomes both his salvation and his burden.

books.title: Cosmos
books.description: A sweeping journey through the universe, from the Big Bang to the search for extraterrestrial intelligence, celebrating humanity's quest to understand the cosmos.

books.title: Contact
books.description: When radio astronomer Ellie Arroway detects a signal from Vega containing blueprints for a machine, humanity must decide whether to trust an alien invitation.

books.title: The Two Towers
books.description: The Fellowship is broken. Frodo and Sam continue toward Mordor guided by the treacherous Gollum, while Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli pursue the orcs who captured their friends.

books.title: The Return of the King
books.description: The final battle for Middle-earth looms as Aragorn claims his birthright and Frodo reaches Mount Doom to destroy the One Ring once and for all.

books.title: Dune Messiah
books.description: Paul Atreides rules the Known Universe but is trapped by his own prescience, beset by conspiracies from every faction, unable to prevent the terrible future he foresees.

books.title: Children of Dune
books.description: Paul's twin children inherit his prescient abilities and must navigate assassination attempts and political intrigue as the ecology of Arrakis begins to transform.

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.description: Ten strangers are lured to a remote island where they begin dying one by one according to a children's nursery rhyme, with no way to escape or identify the killer.

books.title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
books.description: In a quiet English village, Hercule Poirot investigates the murder of a wealthy man, only to deliver one of the most shocking twists in detective fiction history.

books.title: Foundation and Empire
books.description: The Foundation faces two threats: the dying Galactic Empire's last general, and the Mule, a mutant whose powers Seldon's plan never predicted.

books.title: Second Foundation
books.description: The Mule searches for the mythical Second Foundation, a hidden group of psychic scholars who may be the only force capable of restoring Seldon's Plan.

books.title: I, Robot
books.description: Nine stories explore the evolving relationship between humans and robots, bound by the Three Laws of Robotics yet constantly finding unexpected loopholes.

books.title: A Wizard of Earthsea
books.description: Young Ged, a gifted sorcerer, unleashes a shadow creature through reckless magic and must chase it across the seas of Earthsea to restore the balance he shattered.

books.title: The Dispossessed
books.description: Physicist Shevek leaves his anarchist moon colony for the capitalist planet it orbits, searching for intellectual freedom while confronting the flaws of both societies.

books.title: Coraline
books.description: Coraline discovers a door in her new home that leads to a mirror world where everything is better — except the Other Mother wants to keep her there forever.

books.title: Neverwhere
books.description: Richard Mayhew helps an injured girl on a London street and falls through the cracks into London Below, a dangerous magical world hidden beneath the city.

books.title: The Ocean at the End of the Lane
books.description: A man returns to his childhood home and remembers the extraordinary, terrifying events that began when a mysterious girl led him to the ocean at the end of her lane.

books.title: Mort
books.description: Death takes on an apprentice named Mort, who makes a catastrophic mistake by saving a princess who was supposed to die, threatening the fabric of reality.

books.title: Guards! Guards!
books.description: Captain Vimes and the ragtag Night Watch of Ankh-Morpork must stop a secret society from using a dragon to overthrow the Patrician in this beloved Discworld entry.

books.title: Small Gods
books.description: The Great God Om finds himself incarnated as a small tortoise, with only one true believer left, in this sharp satire of organized religion and blind faith.

books.title: Going Postal
books.description: Convicted con artist Moist von Lipwig is given a choice: be hanged, or take over the derelict Ankh-Morpork Post Office and make it work again.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.description: Bilbo Baggins, a comfort-loving hobbit, is swept into an epic quest to reclaim a dwarven kingdom from the fearsome dragon Smaug.

books.title: Pet Sematary
books.description: The Creed family discovers a burial ground behind their new home that can bring the dead back to life — but what returns is never quite the same.

books.title: Salem's Lot
books.description: Writer Ben Mears returns to his childhood town of Jerusalem's Lot and discovers that an ancient evil has taken up residence, turning townsfolk into vampires.

books.title: The Caves of Steel
books.description: In a future where humans live in vast enclosed cities, detective Elijah Baley must partner with a humanoid robot to solve a politically explosive murder.

books.title: The Naked Sun
books.description: Detective Baley and robot Daneel travel to Solaria, a planet of extreme isolation where inhabitants never meet in person, to solve an impossible murder.

books.title: Persuasion
books.description: Anne Elliot, who was persuaded years ago to reject Captain Wentworth, encounters him again and must navigate pride, regret, and the possibility of a second chance.

books.title: Mansfield Park
books.description: Fanny Price, raised by wealthy relatives at Mansfield Park, quietly observes the moral failings of those around her while remaining steadfast in her own principles.

books.title: Ubik
books.description: After a bomb explosion on the Moon, Joe Chip finds reality deteriorating around him as objects regress to earlier forms and only the mysterious product Ubik can stop the decay.

books.title: VALIS
books.description: Horselover Fat believes he has been contacted by a divine intelligence called VALIS, blurring the line between visionary experience and mental illness.

books.title: The Illustrated Man
books.description: A man covered in living tattoos tells eighteen tales of the future, each one animated on his skin — stories of rockets, rain, nurseries, and the human heart.

books.title: The Idiot
books.description: Prince Myshkin, a genuinely good and compassionate man, returns to St. Petersburg society, where his innocence is exploited and destroyed by the passions around him.

books.title: Sula
books.description: Two Black women in a small Ohio town forge a deep friendship that is tested by betrayal, convention, and the different paths they choose through life.

books.title: Wind/Pinball
books.description: Murakami's first two novellas follow a young narrator drifting through bars and conversations in late-1970s Japan, searching for connection in a disconnected world.

books.title: Dawn
books.description: Lilith Iyapo awakens after nuclear war to find herself among the Oankali, aliens who offer to save humanity but at the cost of genetic merging with their species.

books.title: So Long, and Thanks for All the Fish
books.description: Arthur Dent returns to a mysteriously restored Earth, falls in love, and discovers why the dolphins left and what their final message really meant.

books.title: Mona Lisa Overdrive
books.description: The Sprawl trilogy concludes as a kidnapped girl, a razor-girl bodyguard, and others converge in a story about artificial intelligences evolving beyond human understanding.

books.title: The Shadow of the Wind
books.description: In post-war Barcelona, young Daniel discovers a forgotten novel and becomes obsessed with finding its author, uncovering a dark history intertwined with his own.

books.title: Daggerspell
books.description: A tale spanning multiple lifetimes as a sorcerer works across centuries to resolve a tragic love triangle that binds three souls through cycles of reincarnation.
